title: First Class Improvement Signal Capture
goal: Implement #469 by defining a compact improvement-signal contract and making common report/intake surfaces emit signal candidates before remediation routing.
non_goals: Do not create a new backlog system.
Do not automatically promote every signal into work.
Do not make improvement-signal capture a new first-line operating layer.

intent.outcome: Agents can capture compact remediation-oriented improvement signals from report/intake output before deciding whether to fix, route, remember, review, or dismiss them.
intent.constraints: Keep signal candidates compact, structured, and router-shaped; preserve existing Planning and Memory ownership boundaries.
intent.latitude: Use existing report, defaults, and planning-template surfaces rather than adding a new command.
intent.escalation: Escalate if signal capture would become a separate backlog or require host-specific tracker assumptions.

execution.next_step: Archive this completed slice and continue with #467 or the next current #450 child lane.
execution.proof: workspace CLI tests, contract tooling checks, planning surface checks, proof selector, doctor

invariants: Signals are candidates, not automatic work items.
Memory improvement signals stay remediation-oriented and not active state.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #450: make advanced features opt-in through feature tiers and merged surfaces; #467: reduce total operating cost.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #467 remains after this signal-capture slice unless validation proves it is satisfied or superseded.
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #469 closeout, refresh external evidence and promote #467 or the next current child indicated by validation

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: A compact improvement-signal contract is available through defaults, report improvement_intake emits structured candidates, and planning closeout templates can record signal status.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: #467 remains the lane that uses captured signals to reduce total operating cost.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Initial candidates identify large workspace/package/test/evidence surfaces as operating-cost pressure; existing lanes cover command extraction and operational footprint, and #467 can use the captured signal stream.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: yes; workspace CLI tests, ruff, contract tooling, planning-surface checks, proof selector, package planning tests, package ruff, and workspace doctor passed.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: refresh external evidence, then promote #467 or the next current child indicated by open issue state

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Add first-class improvement signal capture before routing and remediation.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not create a backlog or promote weak/speculative signals automatically.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact compact signal shape and which existing report path emits the first candidates.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The change would require a new command or new durable operating surface.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Use existing report/defaults/planning surfaces to capture signals cheaply.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can self-improvement see cost signals before it tries to reduce operating cost?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Archive the completed plan, refresh external evidence, and promote #467 or the next current #450 child lane.
invariants: Signals are candidates, not automatic work items.
Memory improvement signals stay remediation-oriented and not active state.
validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/first-class-improvement-signal-capture.plan.json src/agentic_workspace tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/TEMPLATE.plan.json packages/planning/bootstrap/.agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/TEMPLATE.plan.json --format json
completion_criteria: A compact shared improvement-signal contract is available through defaults.
At least one report/intake path emits structured improvement_signal_candidates.
Planning closeout templates can record signal status found, fixed, routed, dismissed, or none.
Tests prove the default report remains router-shaped while section output carries candidates.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Added a compact improvement-signal contract, defaults selector, report/intake candidate emission, router sample counts, tests, and planning-template closeout fields.
